result.stdout:
Description:
  A .NET tool that facilitates development workflows.

Usage:
  flashoware [command] [options]

Options:
  -!, -a, --about  Show application information
  -#, -i, --info   Show environment information
  --version        Show version information
  -?, -h, --help   Show help and usage information

Commands:
  interceptor  Discover experimental C# interceptors.
  using        Analyze or refactor C# using directives.

commands.payload:
Description:
  A .NET tool that facilitates development workflows.

Usage:
  flashoware [command] [options]

Options:
  -!, -a, --about  Show application information
  -#, -i, --info   Show environment information
  --version        Show version information
  -?, -h, --help   Show help and usage information

Commands:
  interceptor  Discover experimental C# interceptors.
  using        Analyze or refactor C# using directives.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Discover experimental C# interceptors.

Usage:
  flashoware interceptor [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  check  Show whether experimental interceptors are supported or not by discovered .NET SDKs.
  list   Find and list experimental interceptors in a C# project.

  list   Find and list experimental interceptors in a C# project.

Options:

result.stdout:
Description:
  Find and list experimental interceptors in a C# project.

Usage:
  flashoware interceptor list [options]

Options:
  --proj, --project <project>       The path to the project file to operate on (defaults to the current directory if there is only one project).
  --group, --group-by-interceptors  Group the result by interceptors instead of the intercepted locations.
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information

commands.payload:

  flashoware interceptor list [options]

  --group, --group-by-interceptors  Group the result by interceptors instead of the intercepted locations.
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
   Analyze or refactor C# using directives.

Usage:
  flashoware using [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  count <USINGS>      Count and list the top-level using directives of a C# project.
  globalize <USINGS>  Move top-level using directives to global using directives in a C# project.

commands.payload:
Description:
   Analyze or refactor C# using directives.

Usage:
  flashoware using [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  count <USINGS>      Count and list the top-level using directives of a C# project.
  globalize <USINGS>  Move top-level using directives to global using directives in a C# project.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Count and list the top-level using directives of a C# project.

Usage:
  flashoware using count [<USINGS>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <USINGS>  The names of the top-level using directives to count. If usings are not specified, the command will list all top-level directives.

Options:
  --proj, --project <project>  The path to the project file to operate on (defaults to the current directory if there is only one project).
  -?, -h, --help               Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Count and list the top-level using directives of a C# project.

Usage:
  flashoware using count [<USINGS>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <USINGS>  The names of the top-level using directives to count. If usings are not specified, the command will list all top-level directives.

Options:
  --proj, --project <project>  The path to the project file to operate on (defaults to the current directory if there is only one project).
  -?, -h, --help               Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Move top-level using directives to global using directives in a C# project.

Usage:
  flashoware using globalize [<USINGS>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <USINGS>  The names of the top-level using directives to convert to global using directives. If usings are not specified, the command will globalize all top-level directives.

Options:
  --proj, --project <project>  The path to the project file to operate on (defaults to the current directory if there is only one project).
  --force                      Forces all top-level using directives to be globalized when no usings are specified.
  -?, -h, --help               Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Move top-level using directives to global using directives in a C# project.

Usage:
  flashoware using globalize [<USINGS>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <USINGS>  The names of the top-level using directives to convert to global using directives. If usings are not specified, the command will globalize all top-level directives.

Options:
  --proj, --project <project>  The path to the project file to operate on (defaults to the current directory if there is only one project).
  --force                      Forces all top-level using directives to be globalized when no usings are specified.
  -?, -h, --help               Show help and usage information
